Team 2 had a group review where many of these names were agreed.
Specifically, we choose not to call it 'uniqueSubscriptionsPerDmi', since technically all subscriptions are unique. These are subscriptions where there is only 1 remaining subscriber, so it needs to notify DMI.
This is a map. Our convention is always to name maps as "something per something".

The team agreed to this name.
Also it may be confusing to name this method mergeSubscriptionDetails given there is another method on line 114 that merges Subscription Details. This one merges maps of DMI to Subscription Details
I think it's cleaner to just inline this method - then we don't need to worry about names. Though I'm also looking at alternate refactorings.
Not needed if we combine all 3 methods using a single regex

Incorrect data node xpath, it should end with /filter[@id='x/y'].
I would also prefer " to avoid need for escaping single quotes:

    "/datastores/datastore[@name='ncmp-datastore:passthrough-running']/cm-handles/cm-handle[@id='ch-1']/filters/filter[@xpath='x/y']"

Consider making a helper method to construct the whole xpath.

We may need to rethink some of this. It looks to me that if this subscription is the last remaining for *any* DMI, then we notify *all* DMIs to delete the subscription.
I think we need more elaborate test cases (as well as integration tests).
This tuple structure is only used for delete use case. We already have a tuple representing subscription data called DmiCmSubscriptionDetails.

The team agreed to this name.
Also it may be confusing to name this method mergeSubscriptionDetails given there is another method on line 114 that merges Subscription Details. This one merges maps of DMI to Subscription Details
This is a map of subscriptions per dmi.
We have many different subscription event types, it's important to readability to know which one.
Not applicable, as subscriptions being deleted may need to go to PENDING state.
I'm wrong here, it only notifies the DMIs from the lastRemainingSubscriptionsPerDmi
adding a subscription, not an event
it's not an event being added, but a subscription
it's just an id of a subscription, not an event
this is only event publishing, not cache
maybe clarify here that is it for multiple subscribers
NB: Delete a subscriber for fully overlapping subscriptions
